commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1e52968e269df289ba89155c17d1af6ea5fe23bf)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes session write-lock acquisition to cap effective maxHoldMs by the acquire timeout and adds tests around bounded hold behavior.

PR surface: Source +18, Tests +56. Total +74 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defect by source inspection: current main resolves zero min/grace through fallbacks, so the latest PR's timeout-derived cap still records 300000 ms rather than the intended 60000 ms. I did not run tests because this is a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human action is needed because after-fix real behavior proof is missing and maintainers must accept or reject the stricter maxHoldMs contract before merge.

target.title: fix(session-write-lock): bound maxHoldMs by acquire timeout + grace
target.body:
## Summary

The session write lock's `maxHoldMs` defaulted to 300 s (5 min) regardless of the acquire `timeoutMs` (60 s). This created a situation where a plugin hook (e.g. `active-memory` sub-agent in `before_prompt_build`) could hold the lock for the full 5 min while other sessions timed out after 60 s, producing `SessionWriteLockTimeoutError` for every concurrent session that shares the same lock file.

The fix bounds `maxHoldMs` in `acquireSessionWriteLock` by the result of `resolveSessionLockMaxHoldFromTimeout({ timeoutMs })`, so a holder can never block other acquirers past their configured acquire timeout plus grace period. Explicit `maxHoldMs` values below the bound are still honored.

## Changes

- `src/agents/session-write-lock.ts`: in `acquireSessionWriteLock`, compute `maxHoldFromTimeout = resolveSessionLockMaxHoldFromTimeout({ timeoutMs })` and use `Math.min(resolvedMaxHoldMs, maxHoldFromTimeout)` as the effective hold limit.
- `src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts`: added two tests proving the bounding behavior and that explicit low `maxHoldMs` values are preserved.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: added 2026.6.10 entry under Fixes.

## Reproduction

The original error from the logs:
```
Agent failed before reply: session file locked (timeout 60000ms): pid=1302 alive=true ageMs=68305 /home/rev/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/c9ba11be-46a4-40cc-8b29-835a6e066d65.jsonl.lock
```

This occurred when the `active-memory` plugin's `before_prompt_build` hook ran a sub-agent that was repeatedly aborted and retried, holding the parent session's write lock for 68+ seconds while other sessions tried (and failed) to acquire it within the 60 s timeout.
target.author: jason-allen-oneal

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
